project.name: Harbor Health Collective
project.goal: Understand whether community health workshops translate from reach into sustained behaviour change.

assessment.summary: The programme retains most participants through measurable outcomes. Evidence becomes thinner at the lasting and societal-impact stages, so the lower funnel should be treated as directional.

caveats: Indirect reach may include modelled estimates.
Repeat participation is not deduplicated in every source.

metrics.label: Measured outcomes
metrics.context: Participants with an observed near-term outcome

caveats: Outcome definitions vary slightly by delivery partner.

metrics.label: Societal impact
metrics.context: Broader change reported beyond direct participants

caveats: More than half of records are missing.

chart.title: Reach-to-impact funnel
chart.description: Reported people decrease from 7,500 informed to 3,500 experiencing broader societal impact.

frameworkTags.label: SDG 3 · Good Health and Well-being
frameworkTags.rationale: Project context and outcome fields reference health knowledge and access.

frameworkTags.label: Triple Bottom Line · People
frameworkTags.rationale: The available measures primarily describe social outcomes for people.
frameworkTags.caveat: Planet and profit dimensions are not evidenced in the uploaded data.

warnings.title: High missingness in societal impact
warnings.detail: 54% of records do not contain a usable societal-impact value.
warnings.recommendation: Confirm whether blanks mean not measured or not applicable.

warnings.title: Contribution evidence is incomplete
warnings.detail: Available responses describe change but do not consistently isolate the programme's contribution.
warnings.recommendation: Add a comparison, counterfactual, or structured attribution question.

outlook.summary: There is not enough comparable time-series evidence for a defensible success probability or prognosis.
missingRequirements: At least three consistent measurement periods
